title: Key generation logic update request for repeaters

messages.author: Stormlove
messages.text: Feature request. Sorry if this isn't the spot for this. For repeaters/room servers can we have the meshcore 1st run random key gen in firmware and meshcore repeater management phone/web app not simply pick a new prefix at random using the entire range of prefixes and instead do one of the following? Option 1 - easist) The meshcore apps generate a new random key prefix using ideally less than half of the availiable prefixes which we can call the local repeater pool prefixes. We can then use the remaining pool for managed/semi managed prefix handling when a user manually sets their prefix intelligently/not random using tools like meshbud use on repeater control channel on west coast mesh. When changing to a prefix outside of the local random pool the meshcore apps would have a small warning prompt ensure users know what they are doing and verified the key is open using one of the various sources to check in your local area (discord, letsmesh.net, etc) The random function / while loop could be beefed up to only use the 1st half of the availiable prefixes for a given prefix length mode. Not sure how long it will take a micro to weed out a "vanity" key especially if it's a 2 byte prefix. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/73fc9673884236680b8b77de6b590764773edb50/examples/simple_repeater/main.cpp#L81 Option 2 - harder) The meshcore apps are synced with a shared repository somewhere (discord, some online database) with the current state of used prefixes used in your region/location with your selected frequency settings (for non standard frequencies the prefix range would be much more wide open). The meshcore app would then prompt you for what type of node you are setting up local/mobile/semi regional/regional and then selects a good prefix for you using maybe 50-90% of the availiable prefix pool leaving a small percentage for manual managed control. (edited)
